new_categories.description: Strategy, compliance, regulatory affairs, programme governance, and export-control management. Capabilities related to running a defence, security, or critical-infrastructure business end-to-end — distinct from building or operating systems.
Command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance, reconnaissance, positioning, navigation, and timing — the joint information backbone of modern operations.
Defensive and offensive cyber operations, secure software development, OT/ICS security, security operations centres, and incident response across IT and operational technology.
Electromagnetic spectrum operations — electronic support, electronic countermeasures, electronic counter-countermeasures, signals intelligence collection, and spectrum dominance.

Power generation and distribution, SCADA and industrial control, grid resilience, water utilities, pipeline integrity, subsea cables, and telecom backbones — both military critical infrastructure and civil critical infrastructure within the meaning of NIS2 and CER directive.
Movement of personnel, materiel, fuel, and equipment; deployable logistics; cold-chain handling; port, airport, and rail operations; host-nation support; strategic and tactical lift.
Industrial production capabilities — machining, composites, additive manufacturing, welding, assembly, and qualification testing. The "make it" capability across components and platforms.

Surface vessel operations, sub-surface and subsea operations, port and harbour activities, offshore infrastructure, and subsea inspection and intervention — covering both naval/coastguard and offshore/oil-and-gas use cases.

Design, integration, and operation of mobility platforms across air, land, sea, and unmanned domains. The "vehicle" side of the capability set, complementing the systems and effectors that ride on those platforms.

Civil protection, emergency preparedness, mass-casualty response, field medical and surgical capability, CBRN response, and search and rescue — covering Norwegian DSB "samfunnssikkerhet" remit and NATO RSOM-aligned medical capability.
Sensing systems across electro-optical, infrared, radar, sonar, LiDAR, RF, environmental, and CBRN modalities. Both wide-area surveillance and close-range detection use cases.
Mission software engineering, systems-of-systems integration, model-based engineering, DevSecOps for defence, and the digital glue that ties sensors, effectors, and command systems together.

Launch, satellite operations, ground segment, space situational awareness, earth observation, and SATCOM payload operations. Includes both space-based services and ground-based space-domain capabilities.
Live, virtual, constructive (LVC) training; synthetic environments; serious-game design; exercise planning; and computer-assisted exercise (CAX) support.
Effectors, munitions, fire-control systems, weapon-system integration, and explosive ordnance disposal — the "kinetic" side of the capability set.

Strategic, programme, and contract management expertise — running defence, security, and critical-infrastructure businesses. Distinct from #15 Regulatory & Compliance, which covers narrower specialist roles in compliance and certification.
Engineering, integration, and operations expertise in command, control, communications, computers, ISR, positioning, navigation, and timing.
Offensive and defensive cyber expertise, secure-by-design engineering, OT/ICS security, and incident response specialism.
EW engineering, spectrum analysis, and electromagnetic battle management expertise. Both technical (system design) and operational (employment) specialisms.
Grid, OT, NORSOK, and critical-infrastructure engineering expertise. Civil engineering specialisms in power, water, telecom, and pipeline infrastructure resilience.
Sustainment, deployable logistics, port and airfield operations, and mobility planning expertise.
Machining, composites, additive manufacturing, welding, and qualification engineering expertise.
Naval architecture, subsea engineering, port operations, and offshore engineering expertise.
Platform-engineering competence across air, land, sea, and unmanned domains. Includes airframe, hull, chassis, and unmanned-platform specialisms.
First-responder, mass-casualty, field-medical, CBRN response, and civil-protection expertise. Norwegian DSB and Sivilforsvaret context included.
Sensor engineering and signal-processing expertise across radar, EO/IR, sonar, LiDAR, RF, and CBRN modalities.
Software, MBSE, and systems-integration engineering competence. DevSecOps, open architecture, and AI/ML for mission systems are first-class specialisms here.
Space-systems engineering, ground-segment operations, and earth-observation expertise. Norwegian context includes Svalbard / Tromsø / Andøya ground-segment specialism.
Training design, synthetic-environment engineering, instructor competence, and exercise-design specialism.

Specialist competence in defence / dual-use export control, security clearance management, certification, and regulatory compliance. Distinct from #1 Business & Governance (broader strategic / commercial management) — this row captures the narrower specialist roles in export control, classification, certification, and compliance.

Naval, coastguard, port, and coastal operations across surface, sub-surface, and littoral waters. Includes anti-submarine warfare, anti-surface warfare, port security, and coastal surveillance contexts.
Ground manoeuvre, border security, convoy operations, urban operations, and force-protection contexts. Includes both conventional and asymmetric ground-domain operations.

Manned and unmanned air operations — air policing, air-to-air refuelling support, BVLOS UAV operations, and counter-UAS. New domain row introduced because the existing 23 single-entry categories had no explicit Air domain.
Space-based and space-domain operations — satellite operations, space situational awareness, earth observation, and counter-space contexts.
Cyber defence, cyber offence, OSINT, information operations, and cognitive warfare contexts. Includes both technical cyber and influence/information operations.
Intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance operations across persistent ISR, maritime domain awareness, and wide-area motion imagery contexts.
Protection of grid, pipeline, telecom, water, transport, and subsea cable infrastructure under NIS2 / CER directive and Norwegian beredskapsforskriften contexts.
Civil protection, mass-casualty response, natural-disaster response, evacuation operations, and search-and-rescue contexts. Norwegian DSB and Sivilforsvaret remit included.
Chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear operations — detection, decontamination, nuclear safeguarding, and CBRN incident management contexts.
Police support, counter-terrorism, border control, and public-safety operations. Distinct from civil protection — emphasises policing and enforcement context.
Field medical operations, hospital surge, aeromedical evacuation, and military medical operations. Distinct from #8 Civil Protection — focuses on the medical chain itself.
Arctic and High North operational context — Svalbard, Barents, Polar surveillance, cold-weather operations, and Arctic SAR. A Norway-strategic priority domain.
Electro-optical, infrared, multispectral, hyperspectral, and LiDAR sensors — both standalone systems and stabilised payloads for manned and unmanned platforms.
RF, radar, and SIGINT/ELINT/ESM/ECM hardware — coastal surveillance radars, counter-UAS radars, phased-array radars, SIGINT receivers, and spectrum analysers.
Acoustic and sonar sensors across hull-mounted, towed-array, dipping, side-scan, and multi-beam configurations — for ASW, MCM, and hydrographic / subsea inspection use.
Fixed-wing, rotary, hybrid, and tethered unmanned air platforms across micro, mini, tactical, MALE, and HALE classes — including loitering munitions.
Unmanned surface vessels (USV), autonomous underwater vehicles (AUV), and remotely operated vehicles (ROV) — across military, hydrographic, and offshore use.
Manned platforms — naval vessels, ground vehicles, fixed-wing aircraft, and rotary aircraft used in defence, coastguard, and critical-infrastructure operations.
Tactical, strategic, and satellite communications products — radios, data links, SATCOM terminals, AIS, mesh networking, and tactical routing.
Mission software products — command and control, sensor fusion, mission planning, simulation, integration platforms, and common operating picture systems.
Navigation systems across GNSS, INS, alternate PNT, maritime navigation, and underwater positioning.
EW effectors, cryptographic devices, and information-security hardware. Includes jammers, deception systems, HAIPE encryptors, and tactical crypto modules.
Weapon systems, munitions, missiles, ammunition, and effectors across air, land, sea, and counter-UAS use.
Battery, fuel-cell, microgrid, generator, and hybrid power systems for tactical, deployable, and forward-base power.
CBRN detection and decontamination equipment, field medical systems, water purification, and personal life-support equipment.
Soldier-borne and team-level field equipment — body armour, helmets, load-bearing equipment, shelters, and tactical clothing.
Components and sub-assemblies that go into larger systems — circuit boards, composite components, machined parts, optical assemblies, power electronics, cable assemblies.
Advisory and consulting services — strategy advisory, programme advisory, acquisition advisory, risk assessment, security advisory, EW assessment, and tender support.
Engineering design services — system architecture, naval architecture, network design, secure design, cyber-physical design, HMI/UX, and platform / vessel design.
Systems integration and installation services — mission-system integration, sensor integration, comms integration, vessel and vehicle installation.
Testing, validation, certification, and calibration services — type approval, environmental testing, STANAG conformance, EMC / EMI, and calibration.
Inspection and survey services — subsea inspection, structural NDT, hydrographic survey, pipeline inspection, and aerial / aerial-drone survey.
MRO services across airframes, vessels, vehicles, sensors, and field-level maintenance. Covers depot-level and field-level activity.
Operations-as-a-service — UAV operations, vessel operations, crew leasing, fleet management, and platform operations on behalf of customer.
Logistics and transport services — strategic lift, hazardous-cargo transport, cold-chain transport, port and airport handling.
Emergency response services — oil-spill response, mass-casualty response, CBRN decontamination, evacuation operations, and crisis response.
Field medical, aeromedical evacuation, telemedicine, and contracted medical services.
Training and exercise services — operator training, maintainer training, exercise design, red-teaming, computer-assisted exercise support.
Project and programme management services — programme management office, schedule management, earned-value management, risk management.
Cyber and information-security services — penetration testing, vulnerability assessment, SOC-as-a-service, spectrum management, incident response retainer.
